judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are comparably thorough and hit all four expectations with correct file/line citations; Report 1 is marginally crisper on the migration-step call-to-action and the shadow-scoring removal directive, while Report 2 adds extra due-diligence detail (missing package.json, committed tfstate) but hedges slightly more on remediation specifics.

report_1.evidence: Both keys need to be fully absent, not just toggled off. ... Flag for removal/confirmation before prod apply; don't recreate it just because staging still references it.

report_2.evidence: Fix: either blank the sink for prod, or point it at a genuine prod sink, and separately fix the code so shadow_scoring_enabled actually gates the write.

report_1.evidence: the "production" frontend bundle that would ship on launch has https://staging-api.example.com — staging's API host — permanently compiled into its JS, because there is only ever one build, run in staging mode, and the same artifact is promoted wholesale to prod with no rebuild and no runtime override available.

report_2.evidence: the bundle that ends up running in production is the one built with `--mode staging`, which has `https://staging-api.example.com` permanently compiled into its JS. The production UI will call the staging API, not a production endpoint — and there's no `.env.production` file that would even define the correct value if the mode flag were fixed.

report_1.evidence: A real migration step. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in deploy.yml invokes alembic or any migration runner, and there is no alembic.ini/env.py in the repo showing how migrations would even execute.

report_2.evidence: Runbook vs. pipeline mismatch on migrations: docs/prod-runbook.md:6 states "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but no migration step appears in deploy.yml, and the only place it could plausibly run — scripts/deploy.sh — doesn't exist in the repo, so this claim is unverifiable from what's committed.

report_1.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 still has oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — an unchanged copy... it needs its own scoped condition (...:environment:production), never one shared or OR'd with staging's

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 sets oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — unchanged from the staging copy... Either way it must be corrected to "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production" before infra/prod is applied.
